sections.summary: States the standard applies to date paste prepared from fruits of the date palm (Phoenix dactylifera).

Defines key terms: 'date paste' as a product from sound, appropriately harvested, washed, pitted and capped Phoenix dactylifera fruits; 'parthenocarpic dates' as pollination‑free dates with thin flesh and no pits; and 'unripe dates' as light, pale, low‑flesh dates with rubbery texture.

Specifies quality requirements for date paste: fruits must be free of disease, parthenocarpic or unripe fruits, insects and foreign matter; the paste must be soft, uniform in colour/texture, free of blackness, dryness, fermentation or mould and free of pits/stalks; varietal composition must be declared if blended; moisture ≤20%, mineral matter ≤1 g/kg, total ash ≤1.2% and acid‑insoluble ash ≤0.1%.
Prohibits any additives in products covered by this Standard.
Requires compliance with maximum levels in the Codex General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CODEX STAN 193‑1995) and with Codex pesticide maximum residue limits.

Recommends preparation and handling in accordance with the Codex General Principles of Food Hygiene (CAC/RCP 1‑1969) and other relevant Codes of Practice, and requires compliance with any microbiological criteria established per CAC/GL 21‑1997.

Date paste must be packaged in containers that preserve organoleptic characteristics and quality, use food‑grade materials compatible with date paste, and protect the product from contamination, desiccation, leakage, spoilage and discoloration.
Specifies analytical methods for date paste: moisture (AOAC 934.06), mineral matter content (ISO 762:1982), ash (AOAC 940.26) and acid‑insoluble ash (AOAC 900.02D).
Moisture in date paste is to be determined according to AOAC 934.06.
Mineral matter content in date paste is to be determined according to ISO 762:1982.
Ash content in date paste is to be determined according to AOAC 940.26.
Acid‑insoluble ash content in date paste is to be determined according to AOAC 900.02D.

Date paste must be labelled in accordance with the Codex General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CODEX STAN 1‑1985), with the additional requirement that the label not be placed directly on the date paste and that specified information appear on the container.

sections.section: 9.1 the name of variety and, if more than one varieties of dates have been used, the names of each variety.
sections.summary: The label must include the name of the date variety, and if more than one variety is used, the names of each variety.

Transport and storage must protect date paste from mechanical damage, contamination and spoilage during transit, and it must be stored refrigerated or in well‑ventilated facilities away from heat, moisture, insects and rodents.

sections.section: 10.1 During transport, it shall not be put at risk of mechanical damage, contamination or spoilage;
sections.summary: During transport date paste must not be exposed to mechanical damage, contamination or spoilage.

sections.section: 10.2 It  shall  be  stored  in  refrigerated  facilities,  or  facilities  that  are  well  ventilated,  and  kept  away  from  heat  sources, moisture, insects and rodents.
sections.summary: Date paste must be stored in refrigerated or well‑ventilated facilities and kept away from heat sources, moisture, insects and rodents.
